prompt: Generate an image in the precise style of Bauhaus graphic design, 1919-1933. Left half of the composition: complex overlapping real-world objects — tangled threads, scattered tools, layered textures — rendered in chaotic detail. Right half: those same objects reduced to pure flat geometric primitives — circles, squares, triangles — in only primary red, yellow, blue, black, and white. No gradients, no shadows, no photographic shading anywhere. Flat unmodulated color fields only. Strong asymmetric grid composition. Thick black ruled lines separating geometric zones. The left-to-right transformation embodies compression from complex to essential. Herbert Bayer typography aesthetic but NO actual text or letters in the image. No labels, no annotations, no text of any kind. Pure geometric abstraction, flat color, Bauhaus workshop aesthetic.
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated primary color fields (red, yellow, blue, black, white), pure geometric primitives (circle, square, triangle), asymmetric grid composition, thick ruled lines — targeting Bauhaus's core reduction philosophy as structural metaphor for the concept.
